title: Goldfish Growth
description: What size can goldfish reach and what factors determine their growth?
narrative: The user seeks factual information about the maximum length goldfish can attain and the conditions that influence that growth, such as tank or pond size, diet, variety, and genetics. Relevant documents describe typical adult sizes (e.g., 12–14 inches or more) and explain how environment and care affect growth. Documents that only discuss temperature preferences, pond costs, or unrelated care details are not relevant.

title: Wi‑Fi vs Bluetooth
description: What are the key differences between Wi‑Fi and Bluetooth technologies?
narrative: The user seeks a clear comparison of Wi‑Fi and Bluetooth, covering aspects such as data transfer speed, typical range, primary use cases (e.g., LAN vs. personal area networks), underlying standards (e.g., 802.11 family vs. Bluetooth specifications), power consumption, and any notable limitations. Documents that explain these technical characteristics and contrast the two technologies are relevant, while content about unrelated wireless technologies (e.g., NFC, cordless phones) or unrelated topics (e.g., health studies) is not.

title: US Entry WWI
description: Why did the United States voluntarily enter World War I?
narrative: The user wants a concise historical explanation of the factors that led the United States to abandon neutrality and join the Allied side in 1917. Relevant documents should discuss President Wilson's shift in policy, the impact of German unrestricted submarine warfare, the Zimmermann Telegram, economic ties with the Allies, and other political or strategic motivations. Materials focusing on World War II, isolationist movements unrelated to 1917, or unrelated historical events are not relevant.

title: Declaratory Judgment Definition
description: What is a declaratory judgment and how is it defined under United States law?
narrative: The user seeks a concise legal definition of a declaratory judgment, including its classification (statutory vs. equitable), the circumstances in which it may be issued (justiciable controversies), and the effect it has (court opinion declaring parties' rights). Relevant documents should explicitly define the term, describe its legal nature, and outline the procedural requirements for obtaining such a judgment.

title: Right Pelvic Pain
description: What are the possible causes of pain in the right side of the pelvis?
narrative: The user is looking for medical information that explains the etiologies of right‑pelvic (right lower‑quadrant) pain, including conditions affecting the appendix, kidneys, urinary tract, reproductive organs, gallbladder, liver, and related musculoskeletal structures. Relevant documents should describe specific diseases or injuries that can produce pain in this region, their typical symptoms, and any distinguishing features. Content unrelated to medical causes (e.g., food, unrelated anatomy) is not relevant.

title: Social Determinants
description: What are the social determinants of health and how do they affect health outcomes?
narrative: The user wants a thorough explanation of the social determinants of health, including a definition, the main domains (such as education, income, housing, employment, and access to care), and the ways these factors shape health status and generate health inequities. Relevant material should describe the conditions in which people are born, grow, live, work, and age, and how the distribution of money, power, and resources influences those conditions. Documents that merely mention unrelated social concepts like socialism, social security eligibility, or abstract social structure are not relevant.

title: Legionella Pneumonia Link
description: Does Legionella pneumophila cause pneumonia?
narrative: The user wants confirmation and details about whether the bacterium Legionella pneumophila is a causative agent of pneumonia (Legionnaires' disease), including how the infection occurs, its clinical relevance, and any associated risk factors. Relevant documents should explicitly mention Legionella pneumophila (or Legionella spp.) as a cause of pneumonia, describe the disease, transmission pathways, or public‑health information about Legionella testing. Documents that discuss other causes of pneumonia without reference to Legionella are not relevant.

title: Jamaica Weather
description: What are the current weather conditions and typical climate patterns in Jamaica?
narrative: The user wants a concise answer about how the weather is in Jamaica, including temperature, humidity, and precipitation, as well as an overview of the seasonal climate (dry vs. rainy periods) and the best months to visit. Relevant documents are those that describe Jamaica's general hot, humid climate, provide a month‑by‑month forecast, and identify the dry season (December to early April) as the optimal travel period. Documents that discuss unrelated islands, resort reviews, or detailed climate statistics not focused on Jamaica's immediate weather are not relevant.

title: Cerebral Palsy Dysarthria Types
description: What are the different types of dysarthria that can occur in individuals with cerebral palsy?
narrative: The user wants a comprehensive enumeration and brief description of the dysarthria sub‑types that are associated with cerebral palsy. Relevant material should explain each dysarthria type (e.g., spastic, flaccid, ataxic, hypokinetic, hyperkinetic, mixed) as they manifest in CP, link the type to the affected neural structures, and note characteristic speech features. Documents that merely list general causes of dysarthria or discuss cerebral‑palsy motor issues without tying them to dysarthria types are not relevant.

title: Robert Gray
description: Who was Robert Gray and what is he known for?
narrative: The user seeks biographical information about the historical figure Robert Gray, an American sea captain who explored the Pacific Northwest in the late 1700s, discovered the Columbia River, and helped secure U.S. claims to the Oregon Territory. Relevant documents must identify him as a captain, describe his voyages (e.g., 1792 expedition on the Columbia), and explain his significance in early American western expansion. Documents that do not mention this individual or his exploratory achievements are not relevant.

title: Sous Vide Foods
description: What kinds of foods can be prepared using the sous‑vide cooking method?
narrative: The user is looking for an overview or list of food items that are suitable for sous‑vide cooking, such as specific meats, poultry, fish, vegetables, and other dishes that have been successfully prepared with this technique. Relevant documents mention particular foods or categories cooked sous‑vide (e.g., chicken breasts, roasts, steaks, burgers, ribs). Documents that only discuss temperature rules, vacuum theory, or other technical aspects without naming food types are not relevant.

title: Flea Life Cycle
description: What is the duration of each stage in a flea's life cycle?
narrative: The user seeks factual information on how long a flea lives from egg through larva, pupa, and adult stages, including typical time ranges and factors that affect development; relevant documents describe these timeframes (e.g., days for eggs, weeks for larvae, up to 12 months in cocoons) while unrelated insect cycles or control methods are not relevant.

title: Contour Plowing Benefits
description: What does contour plowing reduce or prevent?
narrative: The user seeks information on the specific adverse effects that contour plowing mitigates, such as soil erosion, tillage erosion, runoff, or related degradation of land. Relevant documents should mention reductions in these types of erosion or prevention of soil loss caused by contour plowing. Documents focusing only on contour maps, mathematical contour lines, or generic erosion discussions without linking to contour plowing are not relevant.

title: Salvation Army Origin
description: When was the Salvation Army founded?
narrative: The user wants the specific year (or range of years) when the Salvation Army was established, ideally with reference to its founder William Booth. Relevant documents must contain an explicit founding date (e.g., 1865, 1878, 1888) and/or a statement that the organization was founded by William Booth in London. Documents that only discuss doctrine, general activities, or unrelated historical figures are not relevant.

title: Active Margin
description: What is an active margin in plate tectonics?
narrative: The user wants a clear definition of an active margin, describing its geological features such as high tectonic activity, frequent earthquakes, volcanic arcs, and mountain building, contrasting it with passive margins, and providing common examples like the western coast of North America.

title: RN vs BSN
description: What are the main differences between a Registered Nurse (RN) and a Bachelor of Science in Nursing (BSN) degree?
narrative: The user seeks a comparative overview of the RN credential versus a BSN degree, including differences in education requirements, program length, scope of practice, career advancement opportunities, salary implications, and any bridge programs that allow RNs to obtain a BSN. Relevant documents should explicitly discuss RN and BSN definitions, curricula, licensing, and professional outcomes. Documents unrelated to nursing (e.g., mortgage or credit‑score topics) are not relevant.

title: Medicare Ventilation Definition
description: What is the official Medicare definition of mechanical ventilation?
narrative: The user is looking for the specific definition and criteria that Medicare uses to describe mechanical ventilation for coverage and billing purposes. Relevant documents should clearly define mechanical ventilation, describe its function as a device that moves breathable air into and out of the lungs for patients who cannot breathe adequately on their own, and reference Medicare terminology or policy. Materials that only discuss unrelated payment issues, financial derivatives, or generic ventilation modes without a Medicare context are not relevant.

title: Trapezoid Midsegment
description: How can I calculate or construct the midsegment of a trapezoid?
narrative: The user wants a method for finding the midsegment of a trapezoid, including how to locate the midpoints of the non‑parallel legs, draw the connecting segment, and determine its length. Relevant materials must define the midsegment (the segment joining the leg midpoints), state its properties (it is parallel to the bases and its length equals half the sum of the two bases), and possibly provide a proof or step‑by‑step construction. Documents that only discuss area, perimeter, or unrelated geometric concepts are not relevant.

title: AML Surveillance Analyst
description: What is an AML surveillance analyst and what does the role entail?
narrative: The user wants a concise definition of the AML (Anti‑Money Laundering) surveillance analyst role, including core responsibilities, typical tasks (e.g., transaction monitoring, KYC review, reporting suspicious activity), required skills, and how the position fits within compliance programs. Relevant documents are those that describe AML/KYC processes, analyst duties, monitoring activities, and reporting structures. Documents focused on unrelated compensation, generic software downloads, or unrelated financial roles are not relevant.

title: Thai Daily Life
description: What are the typical daily activities, routines, and cultural practices of ordinary Thai people today?
narrative: The user wants an overview of contemporary everyday life for a typical Thai person, including work, income, leisure, social customs such as sanuk, family interactions, and common habits. Relevant documents should describe modern Thai lifestyle and cultural norms, while excluding historical archaeology, currency exchange details, or language‑usage statistics.

title: SIGMET Definition
description: What is a SIGMET and what information does it convey?
narrative: The user is looking for a concise, authoritative definition of a SIGMET (Significant Meteorological Information) used in aviation, including its purpose, the types of weather phenomena it covers, and how it is issued. Relevant documents are those that explicitly describe SIGMETs, their content, and their role in weather reporting for aviation. Documents that only discuss unrelated meteorological concepts or general weather forecasts are not relevant.

title: Interior Concrete Cost
description: What is the typical cost per square foot for interior concrete flooring, including preparation and finish options?
narrative: The user wants detailed pricing information for installing interior concrete floors. Relevant documents should provide per‑square‑foot costs for the basic pour and finish (e.g., $2.50‑$3.00), explain how additional surface preparation (crack repair, grinding) adds to the price, and give ranges for different finish types such as polished, stained, or decorative concrete (e.g., $3.00‑$7.00). The answer may distinguish residential versus commercial rates and note any typical extra charges. Documents that discuss concrete volume prices, unrelated flooring materials, or basement pouring are not relevant.

title: Swiss Food Popularity
description: What is the most popular food in Switzerland?
narrative: The user is seeking to identify the single food item or dish that is considered the most popular among the Swiss population, based on cultural prevalence, consumption frequency, or national recognition. Relevant documents should describe Swiss culinary specialties (e.g., fondue, raclette, rosti, Swiss cheese varieties) and provide evidence of their popularity such as surveys, sales data, cultural references, or descriptions of widespread consumption. Documents that merely discuss Swiss food in general without indicating which item is most popular, or that focus on unrelated topics (e.g., taxation, generic non‑Swiss foods) are not relevant.

title: Warm-Blooded Sharks
description: Which shark species can keep their body temperature above the surrounding water and how do they accomplish this warm‑blooded condition?
narrative: The user is looking for scientific information about endothermy in sharks, specifically which species (e.g., lamnid sharks such as mackerel, mako, and salmon sharks) are capable of elevating and maintaining body temperature higher than the ambient water, and the physiological mechanisms (regional endothermy, retia mirabilia, metabolic heat production) that enable this. Relevant documents should discuss shark endothermy, temperature differentials, associated energetic costs, and identify the warm‑bodied species. Documents that merely mention sharks in unrelated contexts, discuss tropical fish temperature ranges, or contain anecdotal or non‑technical content are not relevant.

title: DME Definition
description: What items are included in durable medical equipment and how is it defined?
narrative: The user wants a clear explanation of what constitutes durable medical equipment (DME), including the categories of equipment, examples, and the criteria used (e.g., medical purpose vs. convenience) especially as defined for Medicare. Relevant documents should list or describe specific DME items and the definition criteria. Documents that only discuss Medicare supplemental insurance, reimbursement mechanisms, or unrelated medical supplies are not relevant.

title: Exon Definition
description: What is an exon and how is it defined in molecular biology?
narrative: The user is looking for a clear biological definition of an exon, including its role as the coding segment that remains after RNA splicing of introns. Relevant documents should explain that exons are sequences retained in mature mRNA, describe their occurrence in eukaryotic genes, and contrast them with introns. Documents focusing on unrelated gene names, protein sequences, or non‑biological topics are not relevant.

title: Visceral Meaning
description: What is the definition of the word 'visceral'?
narrative: The user seeks a clear explanation of the term 'visceral', including its literal anatomical sense (relating to internal organs) and its figurative sense (relating to gut feelings or emotion). Relevant documents provide dictionary‑style definitions, usage examples, and the distinction between the bodily and emotional meanings. Documents that merely mention the word in unrelated medical contexts or other topics without defining the term are not relevant.

title: Tracheids in Xylem
description: Which plant tissue or system includes tracheids?
narrative: The user wants a clear answer that tracheids are components of the plant's xylem (vascular tissue) and may also seek brief information about their function and how they differ from vessel elements. Relevant documents must explicitly state that tracheids belong to xylem (or vascular tissue) and preferably describe their role in water conduction. Documents that discuss other plant parts or tissues without linking tracheids to xylem are not relevant.

title: RSA Definition
description: What is the RSA cryptosystem and how are its keys defined and used?
narrative: The user is seeking a concise definition of RSA, including an explanation that it is an asymmetric public‑key encryption algorithm, how a public/private key pair is generated, and the role of those keys in encryption and decryption. Relevant documents must explicitly describe the RSA algorithm, its key structure, and related cryptographic concepts. Materials that only mention RSA in unrelated contexts (e.g., conferences, companies) or discuss generic key length without reference to RSA are not relevant.

title: CIS Founder
description: Who formed the Commonwealth of Independent States?
narrative: The user is looking for the individual responsible for creating the Commonwealth of Independent States (CIS). Relevant documents should explicitly name the founder (e.g., Boris Yeltsin) and provide contextual information about the formation of the CIS. Documents that discuss other commonwealths, unrelated historical events, or do not mention the creator of the CIS are not relevant.

title: LVH Causes
description: What are the causes of left ventricular hypertrophy?
narrative: The user wants to learn about the underlying etiologies and risk factors that lead to left ventricular hypertrophy, such as hypertension, aortic stenosis, genetic conditions, and other physiological or pathological triggers that cause the left ventricular wall to thicken. Relevant documents should explicitly discuss these causes or mechanisms; documents that only describe treatment, unrelated heart conditions, or general heart enlargement without linking to LVH causation are not relevant.

title: LPS Law Definition
description: What is the legal definition and scope of the “LPS” law as it relates to Florida mental health statutes?
narrative: The user seeks an explanation of the term “LPS” as it appears in Florida statutes, specifically the Florida Mental Health Act (often called the Baker Act). Relevant information includes summaries of the Act’s provisions, such as voluntary psychiatric hospitalization, criteria for admission, the 72‑hour hold, and how the law is applied in clinical or school settings. Documents that describe the Baker Act or related Florida mental‑health legislation satisfy the need, while materials about limited partnerships, financial reporting, or electrical circuitry are not relevant.

title: Three Percenters
description: What are the Three Percenters and what do they stand for?
narrative: The user wants a clear, comprehensive overview of the Three Percenters militia movement, including its origin (the claim about 3% of Revolutionary War participants), core ideology, stated goals, symbols, notable activities, and relationships to similar groups such as the Oath Keepers. Relevant documents should describe the group’s history, membership characteristics, motivations, and any analysis of its role in contemporary U.S. extremism.

title: Military Suicide Causes
description: What are the main causes and risk factors that lead to suicide among military personnel and veterans?
narrative: The user seeks information on the underlying factors that contribute to suicide in the armed forces, such as combat exposure, PTSD, depression, substance abuse, and other mental‑health conditions. Relevant documents should discuss specific causes, risk factors, or related mental‑health disorders that are linked to military suicide. Documents that merely present statistics, overall death counts, or policy initiatives without addressing causal factors are not relevant.

title: Theraderm Uses
description: What is Theraderm used for?
narrative: The user seeks information about the purpose and applications of Theraderm products, including the kinds of skin‑care treatments it offers (e.g., anti‑aging creams, moisturizers, acne system) and how they are intended to be used. Relevant documents describe Theraderm as a manufacturer of clinical‑grade skin‑care products and detail its product lines and intended benefits. Documents about unrelated medical or technical topics are not relevant.

title: Famvir Indications
description: What medical conditions is Famvir (famciclovir) prescribed to treat?
narrative: The user wants to know the therapeutic uses of the antiviral drug Famvir, i.e., the diseases or infections for which it is prescribed. Relevant documents should clearly state the conditions (e.g., shingles/herpes zoster, genital herpes, oral herpes/cold sores) that Famvir is used to treat or to prevent recurrences. Documents that only discuss dosing, pharmacy instructions, regulatory status, or unrelated drug information are not relevant.

title: Anthropology Environment
description: What is the definition of “environment” according to anthropology?
narrative: The user seeks an anthropological definition of environment—how the term is understood in the study of humans, their cultures, and their relationship to the natural and social world. Relevant documents are those that define environment in relation to human organisms, cultural practices, and ecological contexts, or that embed the definition within an anthropology framework. Documents that discuss unrelated scientific topics (e.g., zeolites) or informal uses of the word are not relevant.

title: Axon Terminal Definition
description: What is the definition of an axon terminal (synaptic knob) in neuronal anatomy?
narrative: The user wants a concise, authoritative definition of the axon terminal, also known as a synaptic knob or bouton, including its location at the distal end of an axon, its structure, and its role in storing and releasing neurotransmitters across the synaptic cleft to facilitate neuronal communication. Relevant documents must explicitly describe these aspects. Documents that only mention the term in passing, list unrelated terminals, or discuss other unrelated topics are not relevant.

title: CDG Airport Location
description: Is Charles de Gaulle Airport located in the main city of Paris?
narrative: The user wants to know whether CDG (Charles de Gaulle) airport is considered part of central Paris or the primary Paris metropolitan area. Relevant material should describe CDG as the main international airport serving Paris and clarify its geographic relationship to the city. Information about taxi fares, airport rankings, or other airports does not satisfy this need.

title: Monotonic Function Example
description: What are some concrete examples of monotonic (increasing or decreasing) functions and how are they defined?
narrative: The user is looking for clear definitions of monotonic functions together with illustrative examples, such as functions that are always non‑decreasing or non‑increasing on an interval, and possibly extensions to set functions. Relevant documents should discuss the mathematical definition of monotonicity, describe increasing/decreasing behavior, and provide specific function examples or sequences. Documents unrelated to mathematics (e.g., hair styling, language reform) are not relevant.

title: Spruce Description
description: What are the physical characteristics of spruce trees?
narrative: The user is seeking a general botanical description of spruce (genus Picea), including attributes such as overall habit, height range, crown shape, needle form, cone type, and wood characteristics. Documents that provide a concise definition of spruce and a broad physical description (e.g., conical crown, dense foliage, flat or four‑sided needles, pendulous cones, typical size dimensions) satisfy the need. Specific cultivar details, growth‑rate data, or information about individual notable trees are not relevant.

title: Hydrogen Liquefaction
description: At what temperature does hydrogen become a liquid?
narrative: The user seeks a precise temperature (in Celsius and/or Fahrenheit) at which hydrogen transitions from a gas to a liquid under standard pressure, and may also be interested in the temperature at which it solidifies. Relevant documents should state the liquefaction point (≈ -252.8 °C, -423 °F) and optionally the freezing point.

title: McDouble vs Double
description: What are the differences between a McDonald's McDouble and a Double Cheeseburger?
narrative: The user seeks a direct comparison of the two McDonald's sandwich variants, focusing on ingredient composition (number of cheese slices, patty count, toppings), nutritional facts such as calories, and any price distinctions. Relevant documents must detail these specific aspects of the McDouble and Double Cheeseburger; information about unrelated burgers, other chains, or generic sandwich descriptions is not relevant.
